top10.introHtml: Top 10 best-buy convertibles within 35 miles of Media, PA — ranked for cheap fun second-car duty. Condition and reliability weighted above raw mileage. Pontiac Solstice/Saturn Sky dominated this batch: multiple low-mileage examples at Great Deal prices. One Toyota MR2 Spyder appeared — rare mid-engine gem. BMW Z3 and 128i round out the sporty picks. Mustang buyers: see the dedicated section below.

picks.whyGood: The NA/NB Miata alternative in your backyard — Solstice is the Pontiac answer to the sports roadster. 62k miles is sweet spot, $7,500 is hard to beat at Great Deal rating. Rwd, cloth top, 177hp naturally-aspirated Ecotec. Simple ownership.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft top through a full cycle. Check door seals and carpet edges for water intrusion. Look for rocker panel rust (common on NE Solstices). Verify no oil leaks on Ecotec. Confirm the top latches firmly to windshield frame.
picks.bottomLine: Best value roadster in the batch — clean, low miles, great price, close by.

picks.whyGood: Just 9 miles away and only 37k miles on a 17-year-old car — exceptional for a fun second car. Price is higher but the mileage is nearly new-car territory for a Solstice. Good Deal badge confirms fair pricing.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top operation and seal condition. Check for tire dry-rot given low mileage / age ratio. Verify oil changes were done despite low usage. Inspect for rust on rockers and undercarriage.
picks.bottomLine: Barely-driven Solstice 9 minutes away — exceptional mileage justifies the premium.

picks.whyGood: GXP means turbocharged 260hp — this is the quick version of the Solstice. Great Deal rating at $8,940 with 88k miles is strong value. Final model year before GM discontinued. Turbo adds fun but adds a maintenance item.
picks.whatToVerify: Turbo boost pressure check (look for boost creep or overboost codes). Inspect intercooler piping for cracks. Top operation and seals. Rocker rust. Verify timing chain stretch symptoms — rattles on cold start.
picks.bottomLine: The spicy Solstice with 260hp turbo at a Great Deal price — best GXP in the results.

picks.whyGood: Saturn Sky is the Solstice's twin under different sheet metal — same great chassis, same fun factor. 30k miles is spectacular for a 2007. Price is fair for this mileage; these are becoming rare at low miles.
picks.whatToVerify: Same platform checks as Solstice: top seals, rocker rust, Ecotec oil leaks. Verify top motor operates without grinding. Check for torn headliner corners (common).
picks.bottomLine: Rarest find: a 30k-mile Sky roadster — collectors item at a fun-car price.

picks.whyGood: Mid-engine Spyder — the most analog, connected fun car in these results. 74k miles on a bulletproof 1ZZ-FE (or 2ZZ-GTE if you're lucky). Toyota reliability means low running costs. These are appreciating — buy now.
picks.whatToVerify: Mid-engine means top down inspection of engine bay at rear. Check for spider (top latch mechanism) cracks — common on Spyders. Confirm which engine (1ZZ vs 2ZZ). Test for oil consumption. Top latching and boot seal.
picks.bottomLine: Mid-engine Toyota under $10k — the driver's car of the bunch.

picks.whyGood: 2.8 is the sweet spot Z3 — enough power, not overly complex. 75k miles is low for a 25-year-old car. Clean title. $7,940 is fair for this spec. Z3s are proven fun at low cost with widely available parts.
picks.whatToVerify: Check for cooling system leaks (expansion tank, water pump). Inspect the soft top header seam and rear window delamination. Look for oil leaks at valve cover. Verify VANOS operation — hesitation on acceleration is the tell.
picks.bottomLine: Classic BMW roadster under $8k with low miles — great entry into the BMW open-top world.

picks.whyGood: 1-series convertible is a hidden gem — rear-wheel drive, 230hp, proper BMW balance. 69k miles is low for the money, Great Deal badge confirmed. B&D Auto Sales is a reputable independent. The 1-series is the last affordable true sports-oriented BMW.
picks.whatToVerify: Convertible top hydraulic rams (common leak point on E88). Inspect for oil leaks at valve cover and VANOS. Check for thrust arm bushing wear (front clunk). Verify cooling system health — E88s can run hot.
picks.bottomLine: Great Deal on a rear-drive BMW sports convertible with legitimate 69k miles — strong buy.

picks.whyGood: Another low-mileage Solstice — 32k miles in 2025 means this car was barely driven. Good Deal badge. Clean title + no accidents per Cars.com filters. Warminster is a straightforward drive.
picks.whatToVerify: With such low mileage, check for dry-rot on rubber seals and tires. Verify it was stored properly (no rodent damage under hood). Top seals may have dried out — operate through full cycle and look for slow leaks.
picks.bottomLine: 32k-mile Solstice that's essentially a stored car — bring a flashlight and check the rubber.

picks.whyGood: Same GXP turbo as rank #3 but $745 cheaper, appears on Cars.com with clean title + no accidents filter. Great Deal badge. 88k on a turbo is healthy if maintained. 18 miles away.
picks.whatToVerify: Turbo health: boost test, inspect intercooler hoses. Timing chain rattle check on cold start. Top seal and soft top latch. Rocker rust inspection. Get a Pontiac-specific PPI.
picks.bottomLine: Best-priced GXP with clean provenance — turbo fun under $8,500.

picks.whyGood: MINI Cooper S convertible at 30k miles is nearly new — the supercharged/turbocharged S model is genuinely fun and practical for a second car. Clean title + no accidents. Cars.com verified. MINI community is strong for parts and advice.
picks.whatToVerify: Power steering fluid leak from EPAS rack (2010+ models). Check for oil in coolant (head gasket). Verify convertible top mechanism — MINI tops can be finicky. Inspect for water pump failure history (common). Test roof fully open and closed.
picks.bottomLine: 30k-mile MINI S convertible — fun, practical, and bulletproof if the PPI is clean.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles in budget — 2 found this run. The 1994 GT with 44k miles is the headline: SN-95 V8 at collector-car mileage for $10,500. The 2013 V6 is the modern daily-driver option. Both are worth a serious look for Mustang fans.

picks.whyGood: SN-95 Mustang GT convertible with just 44k original miles — extremely rare for a 1994. 215hp 5.0L V8 is iconic, carbureted simplicity means cheap repairs. Clean title + no accidents on Cars.com. These are the sweet-spot Fox-platform Mustangs before prices spiked.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95-specific: inspect convertible top mount to rocker seam for rust (known weak point). Check frame rails and trunk floor. Verify cooling system — these overhead-valve V8s run hot in traffic. Test T-5 manual (if equipped) for third-gear synchro. Check rear-axle clunk on takeoff.
picks.bottomLine: 44k-mile GT is a collector's car masquerading as a cheap daily — buy it before someone else does.

picks.whyGood: 2013 S197 Mustang convertible at 64k miles — the modern platform with better build quality than SN-95. V6 305hp is respectable and gets better gas mileage. Great Deal badge. 42 miles is a bit of a drive but worth it for the mileage.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 convertible: check top seal at rear window seam for water staining on rear seats. Inspect IRS clunk (if IRS-equipped). Test MT-82 manual third-to-fourth shift quality (notchy on these). Check for oil leaks on Cyclone V6.
picks.bottomLine: Modern Mustang convertible at a Great Deal price — less drama than V8 but still a proper pony car.
